story.title: Henriette Herzfeld Oral History
story.description: The interview with Henriette Herzfeld, conducted by Ruthanne Marchetti, delves into Henriette's familial roots and her upbringing as part of the Jewish community in Hartford and Bloomfield, focusing on the influence of her father's German Jewish heritage. She touches on her connection to the Reformed Temple Beth Israel and various aspects of Jewish life, as well as her family's role within these communities. Henriette recounts her school experiences in Bloomfield, her activism during college, particularly anti-war efforts, and her circuitous life journey, ultimately returning to live contentedly in Bloomfield. She reflects on the town's history, vibrancy, and cultural diversity, expressing hope for its future development amidst challenges.
